meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve 'Can't Finish Projects'
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your SaaS. Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit, Antigravity, and Claude Code to ship your MVP faster and overcome project paralysis today.
content.answer_block: The root cause of project abandonment is 'feature creep' and infrastructure fatigue. By utilizing a proven SaaS starter kit, you offload boilerplate authentication, billing, and database architecture. Paired with Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform your workflow from manual coding to rapid architectural orchestration, ensuring you ship a revenue-ready MVP.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS startups is paved with 'almost-finished' repositories. You spend three weeks perfecting your authentication flow or wrestling with Stripe webhooks, only to find your market opportunity has shifted and your momentum is dead. The problem isn't your vision; it's your friction.

True velocity comes from standing on the shoulders of giants. A professional SaaS starter kit acts as your force multiplier, handling the plumbing so you can focus on the business logic that actually generates revenue. It’s time to stop coding from scratch and start building a product that scales.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Core Architecture
steps.description: Initialize your project using a battle-tested SaaS starter to ensure you have secure auth, database ORM, and payment processing out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new SaaS project using the standard boilerplate. Configure Next.js, Prisma, and Stripe integration with a focus on a modular, scalable architecture.

steps.title: Define Your Data Schema
steps.description: Use AI to define your database models to ensure your SaaS fundamentals—like subscription tiers and user roles—are strictly enforced.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a SaaS app with User, Organization, Subscription, and FeatureUsage models. Include relationships for multi-tenancy.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'update schema.prisma with multi-tenant models'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Billing Logic
steps.description: Automate the connection between your database and Stripe to ensure every user action is tied to a billing event.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe webhook handler that updates the user subscription status in the database based on 'customer.subscription.updated' events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement stripe webhook handler in /api/webhooks/stripe'

steps.title: Build the User Dashboard
steps.description: Focus on the 'First Value Moment' for the user. Use Claude Code to scaffold the UI components for the core feature set.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a React component for the user dashboard that displays current subscription status and a usage progress bar based on the Prisma schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create dashboard UI with subscription status component'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Automate your CI/CD pipeline so every commit is production-ready, removing the fear of deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Vercel configuration file and a GitHub Actions workflow to automate testing and deployment for the current stack.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'set up vercel.json and ci/cd pipeline'

pro_tips: Always build your billing logic before your UI; if you can't charge for it, the feature isn't worth building.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your auth middleware first—it prevents the most common post-launch bugs.
Limit your MVP scope to one core problem; if it takes more than 14 days to build, you are over-engineering.
Keep your database schema lean; add fields only when you have a direct revenue use case for them.
Leverage environment-specific variables to ensure your production secrets never touch your local development environment.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, immediately set up a feedback loop using tools like PostHog to track user behavior and iterate based on actual data rather than assumptions.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your ideas die in a folder. Use this framework to bridge the gap between 'concept' and 'revenue'. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
